channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Violence reported at TMC offices in West Bengal following Assembly election results
item.description: Following the Assembly election results in West Bengal, where the BJP secured 206 seats ending the 15-year rule of the Mamata Banerjee-led TMC, reports of violence emerged across the state. The Trinam...

item.title: Court Orders Blogger Musa Khawula to Apologise to Julius Malema to Avoid Jail
item.description: Controversial blogger Musa Khawula has been ordered by the Gauteng High Court to apologise publicly to Economic Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader Julius Malema and his wife, Mantoa Matlala-Malema, over fa...

item.title: RBA faces tough trade-offs as it tightens policy to slow Australia’s economy
item.description: The article argues that the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA), under Governor Michele Bullock, is being forced into difficult decisions as it attempts to slow the economy and bring inflation under contr...

item.title: Teenager Dies by Suicide Following Bullying at Army Training, Inquest Finds
item.description: Connor Williamson, aged 17, tragically took his own life at his home in Norwich after leaving the Army Foundation College in Harrogate, North Yorkshire, due to bullying. Connor had aspired to serve in...

item.title: Lessons from trying online financial tips: How to trade safely and wisely
item.description: I no be finance guru, just regular Lagos guy wey dey try manage money better. I don dey watch plenty videos for social media wey dey promise quick money and easy trading strategies. One day, I decide ...

item.title: Nissan announces merger of UK production lines; employment effects remain uncertain
item.description: Nissan has announced plans to merge its production lines in the United Kingdom, though the company has not clarified how this will affect its workforce. The move is part of Nissan's broader strategy t...

item.title: Education CS Ogamba Warns School Heads Against Illegal Fee Hikes
item.description: Education Cabinet Secretary Julius Ogamba has issued a stern warning to school principals across Kenya against illegally increasing school fees. Speaking in Nakuru on Tuesday, May 5, 2026, Ogamba emph...

item.title: FCT High Court admits previous judgement as evidence in Yahaya Bello fraud case
item.description: For Abuja, the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court for Maitama don admit one previous judgement as evidence for the ongoing N110.4 billion fraud trial wey dey involve former Kogi State Governor...

item.title: U.S. National Design Studio appoints Peter Arnell to lead government digital branding overhaul
item.description: Airbnb co-founder Joe Gebbia is expanding his involvement in the U.S. National Design Studio, a government initiative aimed at improving the usability and design of federal digital services. During Th...

item.title: BTS comeback marks K-pop's growing popularity in Mexico City
item.description: After nearly a decade, global K-pop sensation BTS has made a triumphant return to Mexico City, reflecting the increasing mainstream acceptance of Korean pop music in the region. Their performance, hig...

item.title: President Tinubu Meets Global Investors in France, Highlights Economic Reforms and Fiscal Discipline
item.description: President Bola Tinubu waka go France meet top global investors wey include Citibank, Amundi, BlueCrest, Ninety One, Kirkoswald Capital, PGIM and Mesarete Capital. For the meeting, Tinubu talk about ho...

item.title: Sundry Markets Limited organizes MSME Fair to boost Nigerian retail businesses
item.description: Sundry Markets Limited don successfully run the first edition of their MSME Fair – Retail Ready 1.0 on Wednesday, April 8, 2026. The event gather key players from Nigeria’s business sector to support ...

item.title: Government Raises Capitation for Public Primary School Learners to KSh 2,340
item.description: The Kenyan government has announced a significant increase in capitation for public primary school learners. Basic Education Principal Secretary Julius Bitok stated that the allocation per learner wil...

item.title: Oshiomhole urges FG to take economic action against South African firms over xenophobic attacks
item.description: Senator Adams Oshiomhole, wey dey represent Edo North, don call on Federal Government to take serious economic measures against South African companies wey dey operate for Nigeria. Dis move follow the...

item.title: Notable May book releases span fiction, memoir and political insight
item.description: This curated list of May book releases highlights a diverse mix of fiction, memoir, political analysis and narrative non-fiction from established and emerging voices. Ellena Savage’s debut novel The R...

item.title: Western Cape Authorities Warn of Severe Storms and Potential Flooding on 6-7 May
item.description: The Western Cape is set to experience severe weather conditions on the 6th and 7th of May, with authorities urging residents to take precautions. Heavy rainfall, expected to range between 50mm and 200...

item.title: Muzaffarnagar man granted bail in case over social media post expressing affection for Prophet Muhammad
item.description: The Allahabad High Court has granted bail to Nadeem, a man from Muzaffarnagar, Uttar Pradesh, who was arrested last year for allegedly posting 'I Love Muhammad' on Instagram. The court noted that Nade...

item.title: Key witness asks for confidential testimony in inquiry into R200 million cocaine theft from Hawks facility
item.description: An anonymous witness has formally applied to give evidence behind closed doors at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, citing serious safety and investigative concerns. The application, supported by an...

item.title: Hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship: transmission, risks, and response
item.description: An outbreak of hantavirus on a cruise ship travelling from Argentina to Cape Verde has resulted in three deaths. The World Health Organization has confirmed a total of seven cases, including two verif...

item.title: Ruto-Tinubu Exchange Highlights Deeper Economic Challenges in Kenya and Nigeria
item.description: This opinion piece reflects on the recent light-hearted but pointed exchange between Kenya’s President William Ruto and Nigeria’s President Bola Tinubu, where language and national pride became part o...
